1\. Og metadata base url misconfig 🐞 Bug ⛯ Reliability 
Server-side generateMetadata hardcodes absolute API/Site bases using NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars with
localhost fallbacks and introduces NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL without documentation; this can yield OG
URLs pointing to localhost or cause metadata fetch failures/mismatches with runtime API_URL-based
rewrites in some deployments.
Agent Prompt ``` ## Issue description `generateMetadata` builds absolute OG URLs and performs server-side fetches using `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` and a new `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` variable with localhost fallbacks. This can cause incorrect OG URLs (pointing to localhost) or metadata fetches that don’t match the runtime API origin (`API_URL`) used by Next rewrites. ## Issue Context - `API_URL` is documented as the server-side runtime API base (Docker runtime), while `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is build-time/public. - `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` is newly introduced but not documented; defaulting to localhost is risky for production OG tags. ## Fix Focus Areas - apps/web/src/app/papers/[id]/page.tsx[19-53] - apps/web/src/app/orgs/[slug]/page.tsx[14-37] - apps/web/src/app/orgs/[slug]/c/[collectionSlug]/page.tsx[23-60] - apps/web/README.md[42-46] ## Suggested approach 1. Replace `API_BASE` in server components with `process.env.API_URL ?? process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` (or fetch via the local rewrite endpoint). 2. Replace `SITE_BASE` usage with a single canonical origin sourced from a documented env var (e.g. `SITE_URL`/`FRONTEND_URL`) and/or set `metadataBase` at the app root and use relative URLs for `openGraph.url`/`images` where possible. 3. Remove localhost fallbacks in production paths (either throw/log when missing or default only in development). 4. Update docs to include the required site-origin env var if you keep it. ``` ⓘ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools
Updated in latest commits. OG metadata test now validates actual content values instead of tag presence only.

`@is0692vs` ありがとうございます！実際のコンテンツ値の検証への更新を確認しました。テストがより堅牢になりましたね。

This guideline applies to all relevant code/config files (TypeScript/JavaScript) within the project.

_🧹 Nitpick_ | _🔵 Trivial_

**OGエンドポイントのテストケースを拡充することを検討してください。**

現在は `type=paper` のみテストしていますが、`type=org` と `type=collection` もテストすると、すべてのバッジラベル分岐をカバーできます。


<details>
<summary>💡 追加のテストケース例</summary>

```typescript
test.describe("OG metadata", () => {
    // ... 既存のテスト ...

    test.each([
        { type: "paper", title: "Test Paper" },
        { type: "org", title: "Test Org" },
        { type: "collection", title: "Test Collection" },
    ])("/api/og が $type タイプで PNG を返すこと", async ({ page }, { type, title }) => {
        const res = await page.request.get(`/api/og?type=${type}&title=${title}`);
        expect(res.status()).toBe(200);
        expect(res.headers()["content-type"]).toContain("image/png");
    });
});
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@apps/e2e/tests/og.spec.ts` around lines 45 - 49, The OG endpoint test only
covers type=paper; expand the test in apps/e2e/tests/og.spec.ts (the existing
test "/api/og が PNG を返すこと") to also assert responses for type=org and
type=collection by replacing the single-case test with a parameterized table
(e.g., test.each or test.describe with multiple cases) that iterates over {type:
"paper"|"org"|"collection", title: ...} and for each issues a GET to
`/api/og?type=${type}&title=${title}` asserting status 200 and content-type
contains "image/png"; keep the existing expectations (res.status() and
res.headers()) and reuse the same request/assert logic to cover all badge label
branches.
```

</details>

_🧹 Nitpick_ | _🔵 Trivial_

**`setTimeout(0)` は不要です。**

`useEffect` 内で `setTimeout(0)` を使用する必要はありません。直接 `reload()` を呼び出すだけで十分です。


<details>
<summary>♻️ 不要な setTimeout の削除</summary>

```diff
   useEffect(() => {
-    const timer = setTimeout(() => {
-      void reload();
-    }, 0);
-    return () => clearTimeout(timer);
+    void reload();
   }, [reload]);
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@apps/web/src/app/orgs/`[slug]/c/[collectionSlug]/org-collection-page-client.tsx
around lines 87 - 92, In the useEffect block that currently wraps reload() in
setTimeout(0) (the const timer, setTimeout and clearTimeout), remove the
unnecessary timer logic and call reload() directly; specifically update the
useEffect that references reload so it invokes reload() immediately and drop the
timer variable and return cleanup (clearTimeout) since nothing needs clearing.
```

</details>

_🧹 Nitpick_ | _🔵 Trivial_

**`useMemo` の一貫性を確認してください。**

`pdfFile` と `imageFiles` は `useMemo` で計算されていますが、後続の `useEffect`（Line 164-218, 220-262）では `files` を直接依存配列に使用しています。`pdfFile` と `imageFiles` を依存配列に使用することで、不要な再実行を避けられます。


<details>
<summary>♻️ 依存配列の修正</summary>

```diff
-  }, [paperId, files]);
+  }, [paperId, pdfFile]);
```

Line 262 も同様に:
```diff
-  }, [paperId, files]);
+  }, [paperId, imageFiles]);
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@apps/web/src/app/papers/`[id]/paper-detail-client.tsx around lines 155 - 162,
The effects are currently depending on the raw files array which causes
unnecessary runs even though you derived pdfFile and imageFiles via useMemo;
update the relevant useEffect hooks to depend on pdfFile and imageFiles (or
include them alongside files) instead of files so the effects only re-run when
those derived values change; reference the existing symbols pdfFile and
imageFiles (and keep their useMemo as-is) when updating the dependency arrays in
the useEffect callbacks that manage PDF/image handling.
```

</details>

![security-high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-high-priority.svg) ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The `slug` parameter is used in `fetch` request paths without sanitization, allowing for path traversal and potentially leading to SSRF. It is recommended to sanitize the `slug` parameter.

<details>
<summary>References</summary>

1. When a backend feature is missing (e.g., a search parameter), it is acceptable to use a temporary client-side workaround (e.g., fetching all data and filtering) to achieve functionality, with the proper backend fix deferred to a separate PR.
</details>

![security-high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-high-priority.svg) ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The `slug` prop is used in `apiFetch` calls without sanitization, enabling client-side path traversal and allowing an attacker to force the application to hit unintended API endpoints. It is recommended to sanitize the `slug` prop.

<details>
<summary>References</summary>

1. When a backend feature is missing (e.g., a search parameter), it is acceptable to use a temporary client-side workaround (e.g., fetching all data and filtering) to achieve functionality, with the proper backend fix deferred to a separate PR.
</details>

![security-high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-high-priority.svg) ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The `slug` parameter from the URL is used directly in a `fetch` request path without sanitization. An attacker can use path traversal sequences (e.g., `..%2f`) to cause the server to make requests to unintended API endpoints. This could lead to Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) if internal API endpoints are reachable.

![security-high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-high-priority.svg) ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The `id` parameter is used in a `fetch` request path without sanitization. This allows an attacker to perform path traversal by manipulating the `id` in the URL, potentially leading to SSRF.

![security-high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-high-priority.svg) ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The `slug` prop is used to construct API request paths in `apiFetch` without sanitization. An attacker can manipulate the URL to perform client-side path traversal, causing the browser to make requests to unintended API endpoints using the user's authentication token.

![security-high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-high-priority.svg) ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The `paperId` prop is used in multiple `apiFetch` calls without sanitization. This allows for client-side path traversal, where an attacker can manipulate the URL to make the application request data from unintended API endpoints.

![security-medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-medium-priority.svg) ![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `type` parameter from the URL is used to access properties of the `BADGE_LABELS` object without validation. If an attacker provides a value that exists on `Object.prototype` (e.g., `toString`, `constructor`, `__proto__`), the lookup will return a function or an object. Attempting to render such a value as a child in JSX will cause React/Satori to throw an error, resulting in a 500 Internal Server Error. This can be exploited to cause a Denial of Service (DoS) on the OG image endpoint.
